question_text: Three devices have the following efficiencies: Device A = 45%, Device B = 78%, Device C = 62%. If each device is given 1000 J of energy, which device converts the most energy into heat waste, and how much?
correct_answer: Device A wastes the most energy: 550 J
explanation: Heat waste = (100% − efficiency%) × input energy. Device A: 55% × 1000 = 550 J; Device B: 22% × 1000 = 220 J; Device C: 38% × 1000 = 380 J. Device A has the lowest efficiency, so it wastes the most energy as heat.
